title: The reason Russia and China can't win at sea - Sarah Paine
summary: Sarah Paine provides analysis on why Russia and China face structural limitations in maritime dominance. The discussion addresses geopolitical factors affecting naval power projection.
writeup: Sarah Paine argues that Russia and China face structural limitations that constrain their ability to dominate at sea. The conversation examines the geopolitical factors that shape naval power projection for both states. The takeaway is that maritime dominance depends on more than fleet size, with geography and geopolitical position setting hard limits on what each country can achieve at sea.
